remember: Students will be able to recall the different types of share capital, their characteristics, and the various entries related to share capital.
Students will be able to identify and explain the accounting treatment for different types of share capital.
Students will be able to list and describe the various modes of issue of shares and their accounting implications.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of share capital and its importance in the company's financial structure.
Students will be able to explain the difference between authorized, issued, and subscribed share capital.
Students will be able to describe the accounting procedures for the issue of shares at par, at a premium, and at a discount.
apply: Students will be able to apply the accounting principles to record and account for the issue of shares at various prices.
Students will be able to prepare journal entries for the issue of shares at par, at a premium, and at a discount.
Students will be able to prepare the share capital account and balance sheet after the issue of shares.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the financial implications of issuing shares at different prices and the effect on the company's share capital account.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the accounting treatment for different types of share capital and their impact on the company's financial statements.

ncert_objectives: To understand the concept of share capital and its importance in the company's financial structure.
To explain the difference between authorized, issued, and subscribed share capital.
To describe the accounting procedures for the issue of shares at par, at a premium, and at a discount.
board_exam_objectives: To recall and apply the accounting principles for the issue of shares, preparation of share capital account, and impact on the company's financial statements.
To identify and explain the accounting treatment for different types of share capital and their characteristics.
